result.summary: Yugabyte exposes two documented management REST APIs (YugabyteDB Aeon and YugabyteDB Anywhere) plus three CLIs: `ybm`, `yba`, and the local `yugabyted` utility; the documented management surfaces use an Aeon API key or an Anywhere API token.
result.description: Yugabyte provides distributed SQL database products including the open source YugabyteDB engine, the self-managed YugabyteDB Anywhere control plane, and the fully managed YugabyteDB Aeon service. These products are used to run and manage YugabyteDB database clusters across local, cloud, and hybrid environments.

aeon_api_key.label: YugabyteDB Aeon API key
aeon_api_key.setup: In the YugabyteDB Aeon UI, create an API key from the _API Keys_ page. The Aeon API auth docs say any user with the Admin role can create API keys, and the CLI docs say you can then store it with `ybm auth` or provide it via the `YBM_APIKEY` environment variable. See [Authentication](https://api-docs.yugabyte.com/docs/managed-apis/ajgbel79vmduk-authentication) and [YugabyteDB Aeon CLI](https://docs.yugabyte.com/stable/yugabyte-cloud/managed-automation/managed-cli/).

anywhere_api_token.label: YugabyteDB Anywhere API token
anywhere_api_token.setup: In YugabyteDB Anywhere, open the user menu and go to Profile → General, then copy the API token. If the field is blank, click Generate Key; generating a new token invalidates the previous one. The same token can be supplied to the CLI with the `YBA_APITOKEN` environment variable. See [YugabyteDB Anywhere API overview](https://api-docs.yugabyte.com/docs/yugabyte-platform/f10502c9c9623-yugabyte-db-anywhere-api-overview) and [YugabyteDB Anywhere CLI](https://docs.yugabyte.com/stable/yugabyte-platform/anywhere-automation/anywhere-cli/).

variables.name: your-yba-server
variables.resolveFrom: your self-hosted YugabyteDB Anywhere installation hostname
variables.description: Host serving the YugabyteDB Anywhere UI and API.
